title: The Fifteenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## September 1, 2024

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Fifteenth Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time, falling within the long green season of the church year that extends from Pentecost toward Advent. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would be Deuteronomy 4:1-2, 6-9; Psalm 15; James 1:17-27; and Mark 7:1-8, 14-15, 21-23—a passage where Jesus confronts the Pharisees about ritual purity versus internal righteousness. The readings emphasize keeping God's commandments and examining one's true motivations rather than external observances.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church in 2024 was navigating post-General Convention 2024 implementation (held in Louisville in June), which had reaffirmed progressive stances on LGBTQ+ inclusion and authorized continued exploration of revised liturgies. The Diocese of Colorado was in a period of relative stability under Bishop Kym Lucas, though like many dioceses was addressing ongoing questions about church decline and re-engagement in post-pandemic contexts. No major breaking Episcopal news dominated late August 2024.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in late summer with typical early September weather conditions—warm but with potential for afternoon thunderstorms. The city was focused on Labor Day weekend activities and the upcoming school year beginning in most districts. Saint John's Cathedral location on Capitol Hill would have reflected a neighborhood experiencing ongoing gentrification pressures and homelessness challenges that characterized downtown Denver's core in 2024.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

Vice President Kamala Harris was in the final weeks of the 2024 presidential campaign following President Biden's withdrawal in late July, with the Democratic National Convention having just concluded in Chicago in mid-August where Harris secured the nomination. Hurricane Season was active, with Tropical Storm Helene forming and intensifying in the Atlantic during late August, threatening the Gulf and Atlantic coasts. The back-to-school season was underway nationwide with continued debates about school safety, curriculum choices, and book challenges in various districts.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict continued with Russia maintaining territorial gains in eastern Ukraine, though international media attention had cycled to other stories by September. Venezuela was experiencing post-election turmoil following disputed presidential results in July with growing humanitarian concerns and regional pressure. The Middle East remained tense with ceasefire negotiations stalled between Israel and Hamas following the October 2023 attacks and subsequent Gaza campaign.

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in early September 2024 was in transition between summer entertainment and the fall season's television, film, and sports launches. The 2024 NFL season was about to begin, with the Denver Broncos preparing for their season under a new era of quarterback competition. There was a sense of national anticipation and anxiety around the approaching general election just two months away, with polarization high and voter engagement intense.
